metadata.raw_text: {"scores": {"clarity": 5, "simplicity": 4, "brevity_economy": 4, "structure_flow": 5, "specificity_precision": 5, "humanity_voice": 4, "overall": 5}, "rationale": "Exceptionally well-organized chronological guide from preparation through follow-up. The follow-up section (emailing, tracking agendas, showing up consistently) adds real practical value that many guides would omit."}

rationale: Exceptionally well-organized chronological guide from preparation through follow-up. Standout specificity: 130 words/minute speaking pace, 350–400 word target, bring three copies, arrive 15–20 minutes early—these concrete details elevate the piece well above generic advice. The speech structure (hook, problem, impact, call to action) is genuinely useful scaffolding. Language is mostly plain and direct, though a few phrases lean slightly formal ('testimony,' 'formal public proceedings'). Brevity is strong; almost no filler, though the parenthetical on board jurisdiction could be trimmed. Voice is warm and reader-aware—acknowledging nervousness, the intimidation of the dais—but stays closer to polished manual than distinctive personal voice. The follow-up section (emailing, tracking agendas, showing up consistently) adds real practical value that many guides would omit.
